# Week 1 — Ramp Plan

**Goal:** Establish a safe, repeatable rhythm: monitor → draft → review → publish → log.

## Day 1 — Safety + first touch

- Complete [day-1-quickstart.md](day-1-quickstart.md).
- One quote or reply; verify bio and display name.

## Day 2 — Monitoring loop

- Morning: 15 minutes — scan Massie’s posts, top KY political accounts, one national feed you trust for context.
- Post: **Reply or quote** tying a current event to **documented** position (vote, statement, or hearing behavior) with a source link when possible.
- Log: topic + link to post.

## Day 3 — Thread practice

- Draft a **short thread** (3–5 posts) explaining **one** issue (e.g. war powers, spending, Fourth Amendment, etc.). Use [templates.md](templates.md).
- Run through [approval-workflow.md](approval-workflow.md) checklist before posting.
- Post in off-peak window first if you are new to threads (fewer eyes while you learn formatting).

## Day 4 — Opposition / correction discipline

- Practice **correction format**: misquote → quote correct → source. No personal attacks.
- If nothing to correct, do a “context” post: what a headline leaves out + link.

## Day 5 — Engagement without toxicity

- Reply to **good-faith** questions where you can add value with sources.
- Ignore or mute bad-faith pile-ons per team policy; do not feed circular fights.

## Day 6 — Repurpose + consistency

- Turn one strong post from Mon–Fri into a **quote graphic** or **text screenshot** (if your workflow uses visuals) OR a short recap thread “this week in one theme.”

## Day 7 — Review + scorecard

- Fill a first pass of [kpi-scorecard.md](kpi-scorecard.md) (even if estimates).
- 30-minute retro: what worked, what felt risky, what to ask the operator next week.

## Week 1 success criteria

- [ ] No posts that imply official capacity or speak as Massie.
- [ ] Every substantive claim has a path to a source (link or cite).
- [ ] You have a repeatable daily slot (see [daily-operating-rhythm.md](daily-operating-rhythm.md)).
